strategy: Understand the swap. Cast a helpful Charm, helpful Ward, or HoT and the Tree swaps all effects of that type with the receiver; cast a harmful Charm, harmful Ward, or DoT and it swaps that type with the caster.

Turn the swap into an exploit. Load your hitter with blades, then have that same hitter be the one who triggers a helpful-charm swap so the Tree hands over its own buffs, or Feint it and let the harmful-Charm swap dump your Weakness onto it while pulling its blades to you.
Keep effect types clean per round. Because it swaps by matching type, apply only one category at a time and place the trigger on the wizard you want to end up buffed.
Beware secondary effects and AoE. Multi-target spells like Bladestorm swap with the caster, and riders like Fire Dragon's DoT trigger the swap too, so account for hidden effects before you commit.
This is a team fight: 30,060 Health, 148% boost, 69% Myth resist and 49% across most schools, taking least resist to Death (29%). Bring a Death hitter or prisms and one dedicated finisher.
Clear the Wandering Weed it summons on Round 1, set the swap up so your hitter holds the blades, then AoE the Tree and Weed down together in one burst.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 it summons a Wandering Weed minion; plan to clear it with your AoE.
Casting a helpful Charm, helpful Ward, or HoT makes it swap all effects of that type with the receiver, so aim the trigger at the wizard you want buffed.
Casting a harmful Charm, harmful Ward, or DoT makes it swap that type with the caster, so you can pull its blades to yourself while sending it your Weakness.
Multi-target spells like Bladestorm always swap with the caster, so the caster ends up holding the traded effects.
Secondary effects like Fire Dragon's DoT also trigger the swap, so watch spell riders.
